summary.intent: Address four Codex code review comments on PR #664 in fogodev/ars-ui, push a follow-up commit, resolve the review threads, and get CI passing.
summary.outcome: All four review threads were replied to and resolved, CI initially failed due to nightly rustfmt formatting differences in drawer test assertions, a formatting-only fixup commit was pushed, and a fresh Codex review was triggered.

repo: CI uses `cargo +nightly fmt --all --check`, not stable rustfmt — nightly expands long assertions that stable fmt leaves unchanged, causing formatting failures even on otherwise correct code.
The Feature Flag Check CI job depends on formatting passing; a formatting failure causes the entire matrix to skip, making the root cause non-obvious from the job list alone.
Codex reviews are triggered via `@codex review` comment on the PR and signal active review with a 👀 reaction on the issue.

code.finding: Four long assertions in the drawer review tests were reformatted by nightly rustfmt into multi-line form; stable rustfmt had left them on single lines, causing CI to fail.

workflow: When CI fails on a formatting check, always run `cargo +nightly fmt --all --check` locally to reproduce before editing code — the diff may be purely cosmetic.
Resolving GitHub review threads requires both a REST POST reply (using `databaseId`) and a separate GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation (using the GraphQL thread `id`).
Polling CI and Codex review state together after a push reduces round-trips; the 👀 reaction is a reliable signal that Codex has picked up the new review trigger.

friction: CI failure root cause was obscured because Feature Flag Check was skipped (not failed), requiring log inspection to determine formatting was the actual blocker.
Nightly rustfmt behavior differs from stable, requiring a separate formatting pass before push to avoid CI failures on otherwise correct code.
Resolving review threads requires two separate API calls (REST for reply, GraphQL for resolution) with different id types, adding friction to the review response workflow.
open_items: Codex second review pass was still in progress at session end — outcome of that review pass is unknown.
CI checks on the formatting fixup commit were not confirmed as passing before the session ended.
